name: PKK Insurgency Launch — Eruh & Şemdinli
detail: August 15, 1984: PKK launches first attacks on Turkish military outposts at Eruh (Siirt) and Şemdinli (Hakkari), marking start of armed insurgency. Abdullah Öcalan directs from Damascus. Attacks begin four decades of conflict.

name: PKK Ambush — 33 Soldiers Killed
detail: May 24, 1993: PKK ambush kills 33 Turkish soldiers near Bingöl during a ceasefire period, ending the ceasefire. One of the deadliest single PKK attacks. Öcalan later acknowledges it damaged PKK's credibility.

name: PKK Bombing — Istanbul Grand Bazaar Area
detail: July 27, 2008: Bomb attack near Istanbul's Grand Bazaar kills 17 and injures 150. PKK-linked group TAK (Kurdistan Freedom Falcons) claims responsibility. Demonstrates PKK capability to strike major urban centers far from southeastern conflict zone.

name: PKK / TAK Bombing Wave — Ankara 2016
detail: Series of major bombings in Ankara: February 17, 2016 (29 killed, military convoy) and March 13, 2016 (35 killed, Kızılay). TAK (Kurdistan Freedom Falcons) claims responsibility. Bombings occur amid intensified Turkish military operations in southeast and Syria.

name: ISIS Bombing — Suruç (Kobani Border)
detail: July 20, 2015: ISIS suicide bombing at Suruç (Şanlıurfa) kills 33 young Kurdish activists preparing to cross into Kobani for reconstruction. Deadliest ISIS attack on Turkish soil. PKK kills two police officers in retaliation, collapsing the 2013-2015 peace process.

name: Istanbul İstiklal Avenue Bombing
detail: November 13, 2022: Bombing on Istanbul's İstiklal Avenue kills 6 and injures 81. Turkish authorities blame PKK/YPG; a Syrian woman is arrested. PKK denies involvement. Turkey launches retaliatory strikes on PKK/YPG positions in Syria and Iraq within days.
